DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS LIZA BUCHBINDER, MOSHE BARDA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; REQUEST FOR REASONABLE SANCTIONS AND ATTORNEYS' FEES ($300) IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT US BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR WAMU MORTGAGE PASS THROUGH CERTIFICATE FOR WMALT SERIES 2006-AR7 TRUST, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS US BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEMURRER TO THE 1ST COA FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SEE CCP 1061. THE 2ND COA FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPHS 20-21 OF THE COMPLAINT ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS BREACHED THE CONTRACT BY FAILING TO COOPERATE WITH THE SECTION 8 HOUSING SCHEME. WHILE PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS REGARDING THE UNINHABITABLE CONDITIONS ARE CONCLUSORY, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS STILL VIABLE. THE 3RD COA FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPH 32 SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES DEFENDANT'S BREACHES. THE 4TH COA FOR UCL IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING AN ENTITLEMENT TO RESTITUTION OR AN INJUNCTION. SEE MADRID V. PEROT SYSTEMS CORP., (2005) 130 CAL.APP.4TH 440, 452. THE 5TH COA FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND (NOTE: COURT CORRECTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING FROM OVERRULED.). PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS AS TO HOW DEFENDANT PARTICIPATED IN THE ACTUAL EVICTION OF PLAINTIFF. THE 6TH COA IS OVERRULED. A CLAIM UNDER THE SAN FRANCISCO ADMINISTRATIVE CODE IS VIABLE MERELY IF A LANDLORD WRONGFULLY ENDEAVORS TO RECOVER POSSESSION. AN ACTUAL EVICTION IS UNNECESSARY. THE 7TH COA FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE 8TH COA FOR EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS IS A DEFENSE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED THAT DEFENDANT HAS 15 DAYS TO FILE ITS ANSWER. DEFENDANT WAIVED NOTICE. (NOTE: THE ONLY COA WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, THE 7TH COA, WILL BE DISMISSED PER PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL.) (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR WAMU MORTGAGE PASS THROUGH CERTIFICATE FOR WMALT SERIES 2006-AR7 TRUST, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS US BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Chaune Williams, Esq. 510/225-9300 for pltf & opposing party; Tony Cheng, Esq. of AlvaradoSmith 714/852-6800 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. See CCP 1061.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for breach of Contract is overruled. Paragraphs 20-21 of the Complaint allege that Defendants breached the contract by failing to cooperate with the Section 8 housing scheme. While Plaintiff's allegations regarding the uninhabitable conditions are conclusory, the cause of action is still viable.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for breach of implied covenant is overruled. Paragraph 32 sufficiently alleges Defendant's breaches.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for UCL is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing an entitlement to restitution or an injunction. See Madrid v. Perot Systems Corp., (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 440, 452.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 5th cause of action for wrongful eviction is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND (Note: Court corrected its tentative ruling from overruled.). Plaintiff does not plead facts as to how Defendant participated in the actual eviction of Plaintiff.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled. A claim under the San Francisco Administrative Code is viable merely if a Landlord wrongfully endeavors to recover possession. An actual eviction is unnecessary.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 7th cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support this cause of action.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 8th cause of action for equitable estoppel is sustained without leave to amend. This is a defense.
In addition, the Court stated that defendant has 15 days to file its answer. Defendant waived notice. (Note: The only cause of action with leave to amend, the 7th cause of action, will be dismissed per plaintiff's counsel.) (Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
